name: slojka
description: Слойка — AI-first графический редактор (Photoshop-style, WebGL2, SAM 2.1, polza.ai, Claude Code/MCP)
readme_summary: Slojka is an AI-first, Photoshop-style graphics editor built with WebGL2. It offers advanced editing features like layers, masks, and selections, enhanced by local AI models for background removal and object segmentation (SAM 2.1). It also integrates with cloud-based AI services for image generation and editing, and features an AI assistant for command execution.
key_features: Photoshop-style interface with layers, masks, and selections
Local AI models for background removal (ISNet) and segmentation (SAM 2.1)
Integration with polza.ai for image generation, upscaling, and object manipulation
AI assistant (Claude Code) for editor control via MCP
Support for Krita (.kpp) and Photoshop (.abr) brushes
Import/export of PSD, ORA, PNG, JPEG, WebP formats
primary_use_case: A feature-rich, AI-enhanced raster graphics editor for both local and cloud-based image manipulation and generation tasks.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, modern art studio where a digital artist is seamlessly blending traditional painting tools with futuristic AI interfaces. On one side, the artist uses a digital brush on a canvas that displays a complex image with distinct layers, akin to Photoshop. On the other side, glowing holographic projections show AI models like SAM 2.1 precisely isolating objects, and a 'polza.ai' portal generating new artistic elements. The artist's actions on the traditional canvas are mirrored by AI-driven transformations, creating a harmonious fusion of human creativity and artificial intelligence. The scene should be rendered in a clean, 3D isometric style with bright, inspiring lighting, emphasizing the effortless integration of AI into the creative workflow. The Slojka logo subtly appears on a monitor in the background.

name: docker-mailserver
description: Production-ready fullstack but simple mail server (SMTP, IMAP, LDAP, Antispam, Antivirus, etc.) running inside a container.
readme_summary: Docker Mailserver provides a production-ready, containerized mail server solution that bundles essential services like Postfix, Dovecot, Rspamd, and ClamAV. It simplifies mail server deployment and management by using only configuration files, making it easy to version, deploy, and upgrade.
key_features: Production-ready fullstack mail server in a container
Includes Postfix, Dovecot, Rspamd, Amavis, SpamAssassin, ClamAV, OpenDKIM, OpenDMARC, Fail2ban, Fetchmail, Getmail6, Postscreen, Postgrey
Supports SMTP, IMAP, POP3, LDAP authentication
Integrates with LetsEncrypt for SSL certificates
Configuration via files, no SQL database required
Includes a setup script for easy configuration and maintenance
primary_use_case: Deploying and managing a self-hosted, feature-rich mail server within a Docker container environment.
cover_image_prompt: A bustling, organized post office inside a transparent, glowing shipping container. Mail carriers (representing Postfix and Dovecot) efficiently sort and deliver letters (emails) to various mailboxes. A vigilant security guard (representing Rspamd and ClamAV) inspects incoming mail for threats, with some suspicious letters being flagged with red 'X' marks. On a nearby desk, a simple control panel shows incoming and outgoing mail flow statistics. The overall scene is clean, modern, and conveys a sense of secure, automated mail processing. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with bright, clear lighting and a friendly, approachable aesthetic, with the 'Docker Mailserver' name subtly integrated into the container's branding.

name: deface
readme_summary: Deface is a Python-based command-line tool that automatically anonymizes faces in videos and photos by detecting them and applying blur or black boxes. It also discards audio tracks by default and supports various anonymization backends and hardware acceleration.
key_features: Automatic face detection and anonymization in videos/photos
Supports blurring, black boxes, and image replacement for anonymization
Support for multiple backends (ONNX Runtime, OpenCV)
primary_use_case: Anonymizing faces in video and image content for privacy preservation.
cover_image_prompt: A digital artist using a magical brush to paint over faces in a video stream projected onto a large screen. The brush strokes transform the faces into abstract, blurred shapes, effectively anonymizing them. Small, subtle icons representing video frames and face detection bounding boxes float around the artist. The overall mood is one of privacy and digital transformation, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The repository name 'deface' is subtly integrated into the artist's smock. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a vibrant yet focused color palette.

name: rustnet
description: Per-process network monitoring for your terminal with deep packet inspection. Cross-platform, sandboxed.
readme_summary: RustNet is a cross-platform, terminal-based network monitoring tool that provides per-process visibility into live TCP, UDP, and QUIC connections. It features deep packet inspection for various protocols and offers enhanced security through sandboxing mechanisms.
key_features: Deep packet inspection for multiple protocols (HTTP, TLS/SNI, DNS, QUIC, etc.)
Annotated PCAPNG export with process and GeoIP information
Security sandboxing (Landlock, Seatbelt, Windows token privilege drop)
primary_use_case: Real-time, per-process network traffic monitoring and analysis directly from the terminal, bridging the gap between simple connection listing tools and full packet capture analyzers.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant librarian meticulously cataloging every book (network connection) entering and leaving a grand, bustling library (computer). Each book has a small tag indicating its author (process) and a brief summary of its contents (protocol/data). The librarian uses a magnifying glass (deep packet inspection) to read the summaries and a special stamp (attribution) to mark each book with its origin and destination. Subtle digital particles representing data flow around the scene, and the 'RustNet' logo is subtly integrated into the library's signage. The overall mood is one of organized discovery and detailed insight, rendered in a clean, 3D isometric illustration style with a warm, inviting color palette.

name: Pentaract
description: Unlimited file cloud storage based on using Telegram API
readme_summary: Pentaract is a cloud storage system that leverages the Telegram API for storage, minimizing server disk space usage and eliminating the need for external cloud services. It offers a REST API for integration and can be deployed personally or for multiple users.
key_features: Unlimited file cloud storage using Telegram API
No reliance on server filesystem or paid cloud storage
primary_use_case: To provide a decentralized and cost-effective cloud storage solution by utilizing the Telegram messaging platform as the underlying storage medium.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical, stylized illustration of a person handing a physical file folder to a friendly, anthropomorphic Telegram 'T' logo. The 'T' logo is depicted as a secure vault or a magical chest, with glowing data streams flowing from it into a vast, ethereal cloud made of interconnected Telegram chat bubbles. The background is a clean, minimalist digital space with subtle circuit board patterns. Small, glowing icons representing files are seen entering the vault and then appearing as organized data within the chat bubbles. The overall mood is secure, efficient, and slightly magical, emphasizing the seamless transfer and storage of data through an unexpected, familiar platform. 3D isometric illustration style, vibrant yet soft color palette.

name: REAL-Video-Enhancer
description: Interpolate, Upscale, Decompress, and Denoise videos easily on Linux/Windows/MacOS.
readme_summary: REAL Video Enhancer is a Python-based application designed to improve video quality by interpolating frames, upscaling resolution, decompressing, and denoising content. It supports Windows, Linux, and macOS, offering a user-friendly GUI and leveraging advanced AI models and backends like TensorRT and NCNN for efficient processing.
key_features: Support for advanced AI models (RIFE, GMFSS, IFRNet, Real-ESRGAN)
Optimized inference with TensorRT and NCNN backends
primary_use_case: Enhancing the quality and resolution of video content through AI-powered interpolation, upscaling, decompression, and denoising, accessible via a user-friendly interface on multiple operating systems.
cover_image_prompt: A digital artist meticulously restoring an old, faded film reel. The artist uses a futuristic stylus that projects glowing lines of code and data, transforming the blurry, low-resolution film into a sharp, vibrant, high-definition video displayed on a large, holographic screen. Subtle UI elements from the REAL Video Enhancer application are visible on a nearby tablet, showing progress bars and model selections. The background is a clean, modern studio with soft, ambient lighting, emphasizing the transformation process. The overall style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clarity and detail, conveying the magic of video enhancement.
